clinical_history: 53-yr-old woman. What service is she on?
diagnosis: Coronary Care Unit. The ST elevations in leads I and aVL with probable reciprocal ST depressions inferiorly are consistent with acute lateral ST elevation ischemia/MI. Remember that ST elevations like this are never reciprocal but indicate the primary region of ischemia, c/w a diagonal or circumflex "culprit" blockage, confirmed at cardiac catheterization/angiography in this case. The prominent negative P wave in lead V1 along with broad notched P waves in other leads (e.g., V3) are consistent with left atrial abnormality.
